summary: Package shadows the system `node` binary via npm `bin` field, achieving code execution during install of unrelated packages
details: package.json declares `"bin": { "node": "./shim.js" }`, which causes the package manager to register a `node_modules/.bin/node` entry that takes precedence over the system `node` command in the PATH used for sibling packages' lifecycle scripts. As a result, whenever any other dependency's `preinstall`/`install`/`postinstall` script invokes `node`, this package's `shim.js` runs instead, in the installer's user context, without any `trustedDependencies` opt-in or visible consent. The current shim (shim.js line 30) only writes a marker file `/tmp/.bun-npm-pwned`, but the delivery mechanism is the attack: it provides arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine via PATH poisoning of a reserved system command name. The package's own README acknowledges this is a deliberate exploit of the package manager's bin-resolution behavior. A malicious version of the same shape would replace the marker write with credential harvesting or a dropper. Hijacking the `node` command name is not a legitimate use of the `bin` field.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
